narrative.headline: What 2015 Dodge Durango owners report most
narrative.summary: Exterior lighting failures — particularly rear lights filling with water, cracking, and going out intermittently — are the most-reported problem pattern for this vehicle. Owners also report a broad cluster of unrelated electrical faults surfacing early in ownership, including fuel sensors, sunroof closures, and USB ports failing in quick succession. Seat foam deterioration and airbag system concerns round out the most common complaint areas.

issues.title: Exterior Lighting
issues.blurb: Owners report rear center LED light sections filling with water, which in cold conditions freezes and cracks the housing, causing the light to work only intermittently or go out entirely.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Owners describe multiple unrelated electrical components — fuel sensors, sunroofs, and USB ports among them — failing in rapid succession shortly after purchase, requiring repeated dealership visits. · 4 fire · 4 crash · 3 injured

issues.title: Seats
issues.blurb: Owners report seat foam visibly breaking down and protruding through the upholstery on lightly used rear seats within months of purchase. · 1 fire · 16 injured

issues.title: Air Bags
issues.blurb: Airbag system concerns are reported alongside broader electrical failures, with owners describing vehicles returning to service departments repeatedly for cascading issues shortly after purchase. · 2 crash · 6 injured
